questions.question: Analyse Johnsy’s psychological state during her illness.
questions.answer: Johnsy’s illness was not only physical but also psychological. She became depressed and believed that she would die when the last leaf fell. Her negative thinking made her condition worse.

questions.question: Explain how the author portrays the theme of hope in the story.
questions.answer: Hope is the central theme of the story. Johnsy regains the will to live when she sees the last leaf still on the creeper, showing how hope can transform a person’s outlook.

questions.question: Discuss the importance of the doctor’s statement in the story.
questions.answer: The doctor’s statement that Johnsy must have the will to live highlights the importance of mental strength in overcoming illness.

questions.question: Evaluate Sue’s role in helping Johnsy recover.
questions.answer: Sue plays a vital role in Johnsy’s recovery by caring for her, encouraging her, and trying to distract her from negative thoughts.

questions.question: Explain the symbolic significance of the ivy creeper in the story.
questions.answer: The ivy creeper symbolizes life and hope. The last leaf represents resilience and becomes a turning point in Johnsy’s life.

questions.question: Describe the character of Behrman in detail.
questions.answer: Behrman is an old painter who is kind-hearted and selfless. Although he had never painted a masterpiece, he proves his greatness through his sacrifice.

questions.question: How does Behrman finally achieve his dream of painting a masterpiece?
questions.answer: Behrman paints the last leaf during a storm to save Johnsy’s life. This act becomes his true masterpiece.

questions.question: Discuss the theme of sacrifice in the story.
questions.answer: Behrman sacrifices his life by painting the leaf in a storm. His act shows the power of selfless love and kindness.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the stormy night.
questions.answer: The stormy night creates tension in the story and shows Behrman’s bravery when he goes out to paint the leaf.

questions.question: How does the last leaf influence Johnsy’s recovery?
questions.answer: The last leaf gives Johnsy hope and inspires her to fight her illness, leading to her recovery.

questions.question: Analyse the turning point of the story.
questions.answer: The turning point occurs when Johnsy sees that the last leaf has not fallen after the storm.

questions.question: Discuss the importance of friendship in the story.
questions.answer: Friendship plays an important role in the story through Sue’s care and encouragement for Johnsy.

questions.question: Explain how the story highlights the power of art.
questions.answer: Behrman’s painting shows that art can inspire hope and change lives.

questions.question: How does the author build suspense in the story?
questions.answer: Suspense is created through the falling leaves, the stormy weather, and the mystery of the last leaf.

questions.question: What lesson does Johnsy learn from the last leaf?
questions.answer: Johnsy learns that life is valuable and that she should never lose hope.

questions.question: Why is Behrman remembered as a hero?
questions.answer: Behrman is remembered as a hero because he sacrificed his life to save Johnsy.

questions.question: Explain how the story highlights the importance of positive thinking.
questions.answer: The story shows that when Johnsy begins to think positively, her health improves and she starts to recover.

questions.question: Discuss the role of Behrman as a guardian figure.
questions.answer: Behrman acts like a protector for Sue and Johnsy and ultimately sacrifices himself to save Johnsy.

questions.question: How does the story show the relationship between mind and health?
questions.answer: Johnsy’s recovery begins only when she regains hope, showing that mental strength affects physical health.

questions.question: Explain the emotional impact of the ending of the story.
questions.answer: The ending is emotional because Johnsy survives while Behrman dies, showing the depth of his sacrifice.

questions.question: Discuss how the story highlights human kindness.
questions.answer: The story highlights human kindness through Sue’s care and Behrman’s sacrifice.

questions.question: Explain why the last leaf becomes the central symbol of the story.
questions.answer: The last leaf becomes the central symbol because it represents hope and life.

questions.question: How does O. Henry combine tragedy and hope in the story?
questions.answer: O. Henry combines tragedy through Behrman’s death and hope through Johnsy’s recovery.

questions.question: Why is the title 'The Last Leaf' appropriate for the story?
questions.answer: The title refers to the leaf that symbolizes hope and becomes the turning point in the story.

questions.question: Summarise the central message of the story.
questions.answer: The central message of the story is that hope, friendship, and selfless sacrifice can change lives and inspire people to overcome difficulties.
